steps.title: Check current tetragon deployment
steps.description:
Verify your tetragon version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tetragon -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tetragon
helm list -n tetragon 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working tetragon installation.

steps.title: Review tetragon configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant tetragon configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n tetragon -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tetragon
kubectl get configmap -n tetragon -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=tetragon
```
We already have an rpcchecker package to verify Tetragon events from our gRPC API. The next item on my wishlist is a metricschecker we could use to verify specific prometheus metrics during end-to-end tests.

steps.title: Apply the fix for e2e-framework: implement metricschecker
steps.description:
### Description

Implement a metricschecker for e2e tests.
It uses http GET calls to retrieve metrics from all tetragon pods. Note that i keep it simple and:
* did not leverage existing `multiplexer` package since it seems built around grpc connections and didn't want to add a whole new implementation. Also, since we are just using `http.Get`s it is really not needed imho.
* it is intended to be used as a last step in the test (before uninstalling the deployment) to check that desired metric

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n tetragon -l app.kubernetes.io/name=tetragon
kubectl get events -n tetragon --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "e2e-framework: implement metricschecker" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Description

Implement a metricschecker for e2e tests.
It uses http GET calls to retrieve metrics from all tetragon pods. Note that i keep it simple and:
* did not leverage existing `multiplexer` package since it seems built around grpc connections and didn't want to add a whole new implementation. Also, since we are just using `http.Get`s it is really not needed imho.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with tetragon installed or the issue environment reproducible.
